item.title: Stress Test - Every Link Must Be Explainable in Legal Knowledge
item.description: Stress Test: Every Link Must Be Explainable in Legal Knowledge Seed Rule Being Tested Rule: Every link must be explainable in one sentence — no decorative or keyword-match links.

item.title: index
item.description: Knowledge Base Vault “Knowledge is only as valuable as the connections it enables.” Purpose This vault demonstrates AI-Assisted Knowledge Management — a system where an AI agent autonomously: Collects data from various sources (research, conversations, readings, observations) Processes and structure...

item.title: Zettelkasten Method
item.description: Zettelkasten Method The Zettelkasten (German for “slip box”) method is a foundational approach to networked knowledge management.

item.title: Writing as Thinking
item.description: Writing as Thinking Writing is not just recording — it’s a tool for thinking itself.

item.title: Wikilinks
item.description: Wikilinks Wikilinks (also called internal links or bidilinks) are inline links using double square bracket syntax: [[Atomic Note Principle]].

item.title: Weekly Review Process
item.description: Weekly Review Process Structured review to maintain your second brain. Why Weekly Review Daily is too frequent. Monthly is too infrequent.

item.title: The Ultimate Goal
item.description: The Ultimate Goal What the second brain is trying to achieve.

item.title: The Ownership Mindset
item.description: The Ownership Mindset Taking full responsibility for your knowledge.

item.title: The Last Skill
item.description: The Last Skill The meta-skill that makes all others easier.

item.title: The Knowledge Portfolio
item.description: The Knowledge Portfolio Diversify your knowledge like financial investments.
